title: Mapping Workflow
text: This page documents the current mapping workflow in this repository.

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Overview

Genome download and indexing are handled by ./scripts/bcfngs genomes ....
Mapping is now implemented in workflow/mapping.smk.
The recommended entry point is ./scripts/bcfngs, using mapping plan for validation and dry-run and mapping run for execution.
The workflow supports star, bwa, and bowtie2.
For every selected mapper, the workflow creates BAM, BAM index, and bigWig output files.

If star is selected, the workflow also writes:

<sample>.counts.tab
<sample>.SJ.out.tab

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Required configuration
Base project settings are stored in configs/run.yaml:
project: "P000"
path: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq/"
outdir: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping"
organism: "Caenorhabditis_elegans"
threads: 16
ram_bytes: 168632718037
Important fields:

project: used in the output layout
path: FASTQ input directory
outdir: output root directory
organism: one organism key from configs/genomes.yaml
threads: mapping threads per job
ram_bytes: STAR BAM sort memory limit

The wrapper also allows runtime overrides such as:

--project
--organism
--fastq-dir
--outdir
--threads
--mapper
--star-index-version
--bwa-index-version
--bowtie2-index-version
--paired-end or --single-end

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Required genome resources
Before mapping starts, the workflow checks that the needed indices and chromosome size file already exist under the configured genomes root.
Depending on the mapper selection, it expects:

default star: <genomes_root>/<organism>/starIndex/SA
default bwa: <genomes_root>/<organism>/bwaIndex/<organism>.bwt
default bowtie2: <genomes_root>/<organism>/bowtie2Index/<organism>.1.bt2
explicit versions: <genomes_root>/<organism>/starIndex-<version>/..., bwaIndex-<version>/..., bowtie2Index-<version>/...
all runs: <genomes_root>/<organism>.chromSize

If one of these files is missing, the run stops before any mapping jobs are submitted.
If the resolved mapper binary version does not match the stable symlink target, the wrapper now fails before submission and asks you to pass the matching explicit version flag.

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
FASTQ pattern auto-detection
The workflow detects the input pattern directly from the FASTQ directory. The supported R1 patterns are:

*_R1.fastq.gz
*_R1_001.fastq.gz
*_L1_R1.fastq.gz
*.conc.R1.fastq.gz

For paired-end mode, the matching R2 files must also exist.

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Output layout
Outputs are written below:
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/
For each selected mapper, the workflow creates:
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/bigwig/<sample>.bw
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.counts.tab
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/star/<sample>.SJ.out.tab

<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bwa/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bwa/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bwa/bigwig/<sample>.bw

<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bowtie2/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bowtie2/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/bowtie2/bigwig/<sample>.bw

<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/logs/

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
wigToBigwig
changed to deeptools
First the bam files are converted into wig files. This is done using the bam2wig.py script from the rseqc-software package. In order for the automatic conversion of wig to bigwig files to work, one must have the tool wigToBigwig from the UCSC repository installed and in the path. As we are working with micromamba, the easiest place to put it is the path of the environment. In this case the path would be under ~/miniconda3/envs/ENVNAME/bin/. So far I have copied the file manually to the folder, but I have now added it to the environment.yaml file, which is ran during the environment creation step. This was not tested yet!.

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Basic usage

Dry-run and validation
Use plan first. This validates config and inputs, runs Snakemake lint, and performs a dry-run.
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan \
  --mapper star \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping
Example with an explicitly selected older STAR index:
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules mapping plan \
  --mapper star \
  --star-index-version 2.7.10b \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping


Local run
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run \
  --mapper star \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping \
  --executor local \
  --jobs 20


SLURM run
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run \
  --mapper star,bwa \
  --paired-end \
  --project P000 \
  --organism Caenorhabditis_elegans \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --slurm-partition p.hpcl93 \
  --slurm-time 04:00:00 \
  --slurm-cpus-per-task 20

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Direct Snakemake usage
The workflow can also be called directly:
snakemake \
  --printshellcmds \
  --dry-run \
  -s workflow/mapping.smk \
  --configfile configs/run.yaml \
  --config \
    project=P000 \
    organism=Caenorhabditis_elegans \
    fastq_dir=/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq \
    outdir=/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping \
    mappers=star,bwa \
    read_mode=paired \
    threads=16 \
    ram_bytes=168632718037 \
    bw_bin_size=10 \
    bw_normalization=CPM

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Notes

The workflow supports exactly one organism per run.
Unknown mapper names are rejected.
Duplicate mapper names are removed automatically.
The default read mode is paired-end. Use --single-end explicitly for single-end data.
STAR intermediary files are removed unless --keep-intermediary-files is set.
bigWig files are produced directly with bamCoverage. The old wig conversion step is no longer part of this workflow.
Run ./scripts/bcfngs env check first if you want to verify that snakemake, STAR, bwa, bowtie2-build, samtools, and bamCoverage are available in the configured environment. With --profile modules, the wrapper also prints the resolved tool source/version report to stderr.

title: Mapping Workflow
text:
Useful flags

--mapper: comma-separated mapper list such as star,bwa,bowtie2
--star-index-version, --bwa-index-version, --bowtie2-index-version: require matching versioned indices instead of the stable symlinks
--threads: override mapping threads from configs/run.yaml
--ram-bytes: override STAR --limitBAMsortRAM
--bw-bin-size: set bamCoverage --binSize
--bw-normalization: set bamCoverage --normalizeUsing
--jobs: maximum concurrent jobs
--executor: choose local or slurm

title: Environment (micromamba)
text: This workflow uses micromamba to create a reproducible environment with all required tools.

title: Environment (micromamba)
text:
Default environment location
The commands in this repository assume this default path:
~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes
On the HPC cluster this expands to:
/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes
The wrapper ./scripts/bcfngs uses the same path by default. If you keep the environment somewhere else, add --env-prefix /path/to/env.
REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
CLUSTER_ENV_PREFIX = Path("/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes")
DEFAULT_ENV_PREFIX = CLUSTER_ENV_PREFIX if CLUSTER_ENV_PREFIX.exists() else REPO_ROOT / ".mamba" / "env"

title: Environment (micromamba)
text:
Installing micromamba
micromamba can be installed from the github repository.
"${SHELL}" <(curl -L https://micro.mamba.pm/install.sh)

title: Environment (micromamba)
text:
Create the workflow environment
From the repo root:
micromamba create -y -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes -f env/environment.yml
If your micromamba root prefix is already ~/miniconda3, the equivalent named-environment command is:
micromamba create -y -n update_genomes -f env/environment.yml
The environment file states the tools needed to create this environment. We can find there besides the python packages aslo the various tool we need for the mapping and quantifications as well as file conversions.

title: Environment (micromamba)
section: Run commands without activating the environment
text:
Run commands without activating the environment
For whatever reason we might need, it is also possible to run the tool without activating the environment.
micromamba run -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes snakemake --version
micromamba run -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes STAR --version

title: Environment (micromamba)
text:
Update the environment
If env/environment.yml changes:
micromamba update -y -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes -f env/environment.yml
./scripts/bcfngs env check

title: Environment (micromamba)
text:
Health checks
./scripts/bcfngs env check
Example output:
{
  "snakemake": "8.30.0",
  "STAR": "2.7.11b",
  "bwa": "0.7.19-r1273",
  "bowtie2": "2.5.5",
  "samtools": "1.23.1",
  "bamCoverage": "3.5.6" # deeptools
}

title: Environment (micromamba)
text:
First-time setup
./scripts/bcfngs env create
./scripts/bcfngs env check

title: Environment (micromamba)
text:
Keep tools up to date
./scripts/bcfngs env update
./scripts/bcfngs env check
Optional lockfile:
mkdir -p env/locks
micromamba list -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes --explicit > env/locks/explicit-YYYY-MM-DD.txt

title: Environment (micromamba)
section: Use HPC modules for vendor tools
text:
Use HPC modules for vendor tools
With --profile modules, the wrapper now resolves tools explicitly instead of relying on whichever binary happens to win on PATH.

python, snakemake, and curl stay in micromamba
STAR, bwa, and bowtie2 are taken from reused or auto-loaded modules
samtools and bamCoverage are reused from a preloaded module if one is already active; otherwise they stay in micromamba
modules-profile commands still print the resolved tool/version report to stderr
plain ./scripts/bcfngs env check stays JSON-only
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check prints only the tools actually resolved from modules, while keeping the JSON output on stdout

Typical usage:
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes build --cores 20
Using the --profile modules will enbale the analysis using the modules installed via the HPC system. To see the version of modules installed we can test the tool versions:
$ ./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
Resolved tool versions:
  - STAR: star/2.7.11b 2.7.11b (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/STAR/2.7.11b/STAR)
  - bwa: bwa/0.7.17 0.7.17-r1198-dirty (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/bwa/bwa)
  - bowtie2: bowtie2/2.5.4 2.5.4 (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/bowtie2/2.5.4/bowtie2)
1{
  "snakemake": "8.30.0",
  "STAR": "2.7.11b",
  "bwa": "0.7.17-r1198-dirty",
  "bowtie2": "2.5.4",
  "samtools": "1.23.1",
  "bamCoverage": "3.5.6"
}
If we preload a specific module beforehand, this one will be taken automatically into account.
$ module load star/2.7.10b
$ ./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
Resolved tool versions:
  - STAR: star/2.7.10b 2.7.10b (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/STAR/2.7.10b/STAR)
  - bwa: bwa/0.7.17 0.7.17-r1198-dirty (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/bwa/bwa)
  - bowtie2: bowtie2/2.5.4 2.5.4 (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/bowtie2/2.5.4/bowtie2)
{
  "snakemake": "8.30.0",
  "STAR": "2.7.10b",
  "bwa": "0.7.17-r1198-dirty",
  "bowtie2": "2.5.4",
  "samtools": "1.23.1",
  "bamCoverage": "3.5.6"
}
If you preload an older mapper module, the wrapper will reuse it and require you to select the matching versioned index explicitly in mapping:
module load star/2.7.10b
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules mapping plan \
  --mapper star \
  --star-index-version 2.7.10b
If the needed genome index for this version is not available, it will resultsin an error message and the run will be aborted:
$ ./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules mapping plan   --mapper star   --star-index-version 2.7.10b
Resolved tool versions:
  - python: micromamba 3.12.13 (/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes/bin/python)
  - snakemake: micromamba 8.30.0 (/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes/bin/snakemake)
  - samtools: micromamba 1.23.1 (/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes/bin/samtools)
  - bamCoverage: micromamba 3.5.6 (/fs/gpfs41/lv07/fileset07/home/b_cox/yeroslaviz/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes/bin/bamCoverage)
  - STAR: star/2.7.10b 2.7.10b (/fs/pool/pool-cox-modules-software/STAR/2.7.10b/STAR)
Missing required STAR index resource: /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/Caenorhabditis_elegans/starIndex-2.7.10b/SA

Demultiplexing with bcl-convert
This repo does not automate demultiplexing yet. Typical usage on the cluster is:
module load bclconvert/4.3.13
bcl-convert --help

title: Environment (micromamba)
text:
Missing tools
If ./scripts/bcfngs env check reports a missing tool:

Update the environment:

./scripts/bcfngs env update
./scripts/bcfngs env check

If you’re using a non-default location for your enviroonment, pass it explicitly:

./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /path/to/your/env env update
./scripts/bcfngs --env-prefix /path/to/your/env env check

Verify the package is installed in the expected environment:

micromamba list -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes | grep -Ei 'snakemake|star|bwa|bowtie2|samtools|rseqc'
micromamba run -p ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes snakemake --version

If needed, recreate the environment:

rm -rf ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes
./scripts/bcfngs env create
./scripts/bcfngs env check

title: HOWTO
text: This is an internal HOW-TO for the workflow of the automation for the bcfngs core facility.

title: HOWTO
text:
genome indexing
This steps needs to be run only once. unless new genomes are added.
This is all run on the hpcl5002.

log-in to the server.
If not done before, activate the conda environment inside a screen, so that one can log out from the server and still run the workflow in the background. More information about the is on the github web site.
open the config.yaml file, e.g. vi /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/scripts/config.yaml and add the genome links from the ensembl FTP site in this format:

  Dps.3.0.45:
    fasta: "ftp://ftp.ensemblgenomes.org/pub/metazoa/release-45/fasta/drosophila_pseudoobscura/dna/Drosophila_pseudoobscura.Dpse_3.0.dna.toplevel.fa.gz"
    gtf: "ftp://ftp.ensemblgenomes.org/pub/metazoa/release-45/gtf/drosophila_pseudoobscura/Drosophila_pseudoobscura.Dpse_3.0.45.gtf.gz"

run the command snakemake -nps ../scripts/getGenome_IndexGenome.Snakefile to check that the new genome is added to the workflow (the -n is a dry-run which just output the command without performing the tasks.)
run the same command without the -n - snakemake -ps ../scripts/getGenome_IndexGenome.Snakefile -j 12. This will download the fastA and gtf files from the given links and create the index files for them as well. The parameter -j sets the number pf cores used in the snakemake run. If the number of cores is smaller then the one sets for e.g. STAR, only this numner of cores will be used.

title: HOWTO
text:
Mapping
Mapping will be done with the STAR aligner for a specific genome either in a paired-end or single-end format.

Changing parameters before mapping
The following setting must be adjusted before running the script:
######## Project Setting
# To create a separate project results folder for each run, change the name of the project here.
project: "P152"
# this path would point to the raw data in fastq format. This must be changed each time for the correct data to be analyzed.
path: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P146/P152_ChIP_Asx_5_6/conc.fastq"
# Which organism to map the data to
org: "Dme.BDGP6.22"
The project and the path should point to the current project which is being mapped.
the org must be the organism the data is mapped against. The term must be identical to the term in the list of organisms from the config files.


running the star mapping step.
first, change the working directory to the path of the actual project on the pool-bcfngs. e.g.
cd /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq/
Next run the snakemake script first as a dry-run (-n) and if all seems well than run it for real.
e.g.
# for single-end samples
snakemake -nps /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/scripts/Star.MappingQuant.SingleEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50
# for paired-end samples
snakemake -ps /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/scripts/Star.MappingQuant.PairedEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
This page is the operator guide for ./scripts/bcfngs genomes. It covers:
Environment setup is documented elsewhere. This page assumes ./scripts/bcfngs already runs on your machine or cluster.

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
Command overview
bcfngs genomes currently exposes three subcommands:

check-updates: compare the pinned Ensembl release in configs/genomes.yaml with the latest Ensembl release visible on ftp.ensembl.org
plan: run preflight checks, snakemake --lint, and a dry-run
build: run plan, then execute the workflow for real

Inspect the wrapper help first. Current output from the script:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes --help
usage: bcfngs genomes [-h] {check-updates,plan,build} ...

positional arguments:
  {check-updates,plan,build}

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan --help
usage: bcfngs genomes plan [-h] [--organism ORGANISM]
                           [--star-index-version STAR_INDEX_VERSION]
                           [--bwa-index-version BWA_INDEX_VERSION]
                           [--bowtie2-index-version BOWTIE2_INDEX_VERSION]

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --organism ORGANISM   Comma-separated subset of organism keys (e.g.
                        Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus)
  --star-index-version STAR_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this STAR index version label.
  --bwa-index-version BWA_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this bwa index version label.
  --bowtie2-index-version BOWTIE2_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this bowtie2 index version label.
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --help
usage: bcfngs genomes build [-h] [--executor {local,slurm}] [--cores CORES]
                            [--jobs JOBS] [--organism ORGANISM]
                            [--star-index-version STAR_INDEX_VERSION]
                            [--bwa-index-version BWA_INDEX_VERSION]
                            [--bowtie2-index-version BOWTIE2_INDEX_VERSION]
                            [-no-skip-plan]

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --executor {local,slurm}
  --cores CORES
  --jobs JOBS
  --organism ORGANISM   Comma-separated subset of organism keys (e.g.
                        Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus)
  --star-index-version STAR_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this STAR index version label.
  --bwa-index-version BWA_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this bwa index version label.
  --bowtie2-index-version BOWTIE2_INDEX_VERSION
                        Require/use this bowtie2 index version label.
  -no-skip-plan, --no-skip-plan
                        Run plan preflight checks before build (config
                        validation, URL checks, lint, dry-run).
./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates --help
usage: bcfngs genomes check-updates [-h]

optional arguments:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
That means:

use --cores to control total workflow parallelism
use --organism for a comma-separated subset of organism keys
use --star-index-version, --bwa-index-version, and --bowtie2-index-version when the resolved tool version should target a matching versioned index explicitly
use direct snakemake for advanced cases such as scheduler memory flags or building only one index type

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
Shared wrapper parameters
These apply to all three genomes subcommands and are cross-referenced from the chapters below.



Parameter
Current default
Applies to
Notes




--profile
micromamba
check-updates, plan, build
modules now resolves STAR, bwa, and bowtie2 from modules while keeping python, snakemake, and curl in micromamba.


--env-prefix
./.mamba/env
check-updates, plan, build
This is the current default in scripts/bcfngs. Override it if your environment lives elsewhere.


configs/genomes.yaml
required
check-updates, plan, build
Release pins, organisms, output root, thread defaults, RAM defaults, and index version strings all come from this file.

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
Common config fields
These are the main configs/genomes.yaml fields referenced by the three command chapters.









Config field
Current value
Used by
Notes




genomes_root
/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes
plan, build
plan checks write access here. build writes sources, indices, stable symlinks, and per-organism manifests here.


releases.ensembl
116
check-updates, plan, build
Main Ensembl release pin. check-updates compares this to ftp.ensembl.org.


releases.ensemblgenomes.metazoa
61
plan, build
Used for ensemblgenomes organisms such as Drosophila_pseudoobscura.


default_threads
16
build
Used by STAR genome generation and bowtie2-build.


default_ram_bytes
168632718037
build
Passed to STAR --limitGenomeGenerateRAM.


index_versions.star
"unknown"
build
Controls versioned output paths such as starIndex-<version>.


index_versions.bwa
"unknown"
build
Controls versioned output paths such as bwaIndex-<version>.


index_versions.bowtie2
"unknown"
build
Controls versioned output paths such as bowtie2Index-<version>.


organisms.*
all configured organisms
plan, build
Scientific-name keys such as Homo_sapiens, Mus_musculus, Drosophila_melanogaster.


organisms.*.sa_index
organism-specific
build
Passed to STAR --genomeSAindexNbases.


organisms.*.fasta_url / gtf_url
optional explicit URLs
plan, build
Used for organisms that do not fit the generated Ensembl filename pattern.

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
check-updates

Parameters and defaults



Item
Current default / value
Where set
Notes




Shared wrapper parameters
see Shared wrapper parameters
CLI
--profile and --env-prefix still apply here.


Command-specific flags
none
CLI
This subcommand has no extra runtime flags beyond -h/--help.


Compared release pin
releases.ensembl: 116
configs/genomes.yaml
This is the pinned release compared to the latest Ensembl release found on https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/.


Network target
https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/
internal behavior
If it is unreachable, the command reports the current pin but cannot confirm the latest release.


Writes to disk
none
internal behavior
Report only. No files are edited.


Not checked automatically
releases.ensemblgenomes.*, explicit fasta_url, explicit gtf_url
config
Review these manually when working with EnsemblGenomes or custom URLs.





Micromamba / conda-style environment
Use this when the wrapper should run in its default micromamba profile:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates
Typical output looks like:
Ensembl latest release: 117  current pinned: 116  status: UPDATE_AVAILABLE
Suggested bump: set releases.ensembl to 117 in configs/genomes.yaml


Modules profile
If you want the same command to run under the modules profile:
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes check-updates
For check-updates, the modules profile usually does not change the result. It is only relevant if you are keeping your operational workflow consistent across all genomes commands.


If an update is available

Edit configs/genomes.yaml.
Bump releases.ensembl, for example 116 to 117.
Review any organism entries that use explicit fasta_url or gtf_url.
Continue with plan and then build.

If you are updating an EnsemblGenomes organism such as Drosophila_pseudoobscura, also review releases.ensemblgenomes plus the explicit URLs in its organism block.

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
plan

Parameters and defaults



Item
Current default / value
Where set
Notes




Shared wrapper parameters
see Shared wrapper parameters
CLI
--profile and --env-prefix apply here.


Command-specific flags
--organism, --star-index-version, --bwa-index-version, --bowtie2-index-version
CLI
Use these to dry-run a subset or validate an explicit tool/index version pairing.


Organism scope
all configured organisms
wrapper behavior
The wrapper plans the full configured set. Use direct snakemake with --config org_subset=... if you want a subset dry-run.


genomes_root
/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes
configs/genomes.yaml
plan checks that this location is writable.


Release pins and URLs
see Common config fields
config
plan resolves all FASTA/GTF URLs from the current release pins and organism blocks, then tests them with curl -fsSI.


Underlying Snakemake steps
snakemake --lint then snakemake -npr
wrapper behavior
plan is both a lint pass and a dry-run.





Local run with micromamba / conda-style environment
Use this before every real build:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
This checks that:

configs/genomes.yaml parses
genomes_root is writable
each configured FASTA/GTF URL responds
workflow/genomes.smk passes snakemake --lint
the workflow dry-run resolves successfully



Local run with modules
If your cluster provides aligners via modules:
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes plan
The modules profile now resolves mapper binaries explicitly. python, snakemake, and curl stay in micromamba, while STAR, bwa, and bowtie2 are reused or loaded from modules. Each top-level command prints the resolved tool/version report to stderr.


Direct Snakemake subset dry-run
The wrapper does not expose a --organism option for plan. Keep the direct snakemake form when you need a subset dry-run:
snakemake -npr \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  --config org_subset=Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus
This is useful for:

testing a release bump on one organism first
checking a new organism block before rebuilding everything
isolating failures in one species without noise from the rest of the config



SLURM-oriented dry-run example
For clusters where you want to inspect scheduler-facing settings before a real run, keep the direct snakemake example:
snakemake -npr \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --default-resources mem_mb=180000 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml
plan itself does not submit jobs in dry-run mode, but this is still useful to check that your SLURM executor configuration parses correctly.

title: Updating genomes and indices
text:
build

Parameters and defaults



Item
Current default / value
Where set
Notes




Shared wrapper parameters
see Shared wrapper parameters
CLI
--profile and --env-prefix apply here too.


--cores
16
CLI
Total Snakemake core budget for ./scripts/bcfngs genomes build.


--organism
all configured organisms
CLI
Optional comma-separated subset such as Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus.


--star-index-version, --bwa-index-version, --bowtie2-index-version
resolved binary version
CLI
If set, each flag must match the actual resolved binary version for that tool.


default_threads
16
configs/genomes.yaml
Used by STAR genome generation and bowtie2-build. This is separate from --cores.


default_ram_bytes
168632718037
configs/genomes.yaml
Passed to STAR --limitGenomeGenerateRAM.


index_versions.*
resolved at runtime
wrapper behavior
Controls versioned output paths such as starIndex-<version>.


organisms.*.sa_index
organism-specific
configs/genomes.yaml
Controls STAR suffix-array sizing.


Stable symlink outputs
starIndex, bwaIndex, bowtie2Index
wrapper behavior
Repointed after build to the highest built version available for that organism and tool.


Per-organism manifest
index-manifest.json
wrapper behavior
Written under each organism directory with assembly, source release, tool versions, stable targets, and discovered versioned builds.


Advanced scheduler knobs
direct snakemake only
CLI at Snakemake level
Use these for SLURM executor settings, mem_mb, or mem_mb_per_cpu.





Local run with micromamba / conda-style environment
Build all configured genomes:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
More local examples:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 8
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 32
--cores is the total Snakemake job budget. It is not the same thing as default_threads, which controls per-tool threading inside rules such as STAR genome generation.


Local run with modules
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes build --cores 20
This is the best fit when the aligners should come from modules but python and snakemake should still come from the micromamba environment.


Build only selected organisms
Organism names must match the keys in configs/genomes.yaml exactly.
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 12 --organism Drosophila_melanogaster,Drosophila_pseudoobscura
Use this when:

you added or changed only one organism
you want to test a release bump on a smaller subset first
you do not want to rebuild every organism after a config edit



SLURM-oriented build example
The wrapper does not expose --executor, --mem, or --mem-per-cpu. Keep the direct snakemake form for cluster-scheduled runs:
snakemake -pr \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --default-resources mem_mb=180000 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml
If your site schedules by memory per CPU instead:
snakemake -pr \
  --executor slurm \
  --jobs 20 \
  --default-resources mem_mb_per_cpu=8000 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml
Adjust these examples to your local cluster conventions. The current ./scripts/bcfngs genomes build command does not parse --mem or --mem-per-cpu directly.


Memory and threading
There are two different layers to tune:

--cores: total workflow parallelism for the outer Snakemake run
default_threads: per-rule thread count used inside STAR genome generation and bowtie2-build

Typical patterns:

20-core workstation, one large STAR job at a time: set default_threads: 20 and run ./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
20-core workstation, allow two medium jobs in parallel: set default_threads: 10 and run ./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
larger-memory node for STAR genome generation: increase default_ram_bytes

For a permanent STAR memory change, edit:
default_ram_bytes: 250000000000


Build only one index type with direct Snakemake
The wrapper always targets the full workflow. To build only one tool, target the desired output file directly.
Replace each version placeholder with the matching value from index_versions in configs/genomes.yaml.
Only STAR for Homo_sapiens:
snakemake -pr -j 20 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/Homo_sapiens/starIndex-<STAR_VERSION>/SA
Only bwa for Homo_sapiens:
snakemake -pr -j 20 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/Homo_sapiens/bwaIndex-<BWA_VERSION>/Homo_sapiens.bwt
Only bowtie2 for Homo_sapiens:
snakemake -pr -j 20 \
  -s workflow/genomes.smk \
  --configfile configs/genomes.yaml \
  /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/Homo_sapiens/bowtie2Index-<BOWTIE2_VERSION>/Homo_sapiens.1.bt2
These commands still pull in prerequisite downloads, but they avoid rebuilding every index type.


Rebuild after a genome release change
After check-updates reports a newer Ensembl release:

Edit configs/genomes.yaml.
Bump releases.ensembl, for example 116 to 117.
Review any explicit fasta_url or gtf_url entries.
Run plan.
Run build.

If you want a cautious rollout, start with one organism:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens


Rebuild after a tool version change
When the genome sequence did not change but an indexer changed, update only the relevant version labels:
index_versions:
  star: "2.7.11b"
  bwa: "0.7.18"
  bowtie2: "2.5.5"
Then rerun:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
Successful builds update the stable symlinks automatically to the highest built version detected on disk:

/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/starIndex
/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/bwaIndex
/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes/<Organism>/bowtie2Index

These stable names point to versioned directories such as starIndex-2.7.11b.
Each organism directory also gets an index-manifest.json file recording:

the assembly and source URLs
the Ensembl or EnsemblGenomes release used for indexing
the resolved tool versions used during the last build command
the current stable symlink targets
the discovered versioned index directories for STAR, bwa, and bowtie2

title: Genome update and mapping workflow
text:
This project contains a conda-snakemake workflow for the mapping of the concatenated fastq file sequenced at the NGS facility of the MPIB.
The following documentation was meant for the sequencing center to help them run a preliminary analysis on the sequenced samples.
Following steps will be cover in this documentation

Instruction on how to create the conda environment with the needed tools for the mapping and genomic indices updates
The snakemake workflow for updating the genomic indices needed for the mapping
Automation of themapping procedure using different mapping tools (Some tools are not yet incorporated in the workflow):


STAR
bowtie2
bwa
kallisto
salmon
cellranger

title: Genome update and mapping workflow
text:
Preparing the genomeic indicies
For the genomic indices we need the chromosome sequences as fastA file as well as the genomic features in a gtf format. The bcfngs genomes script can download the genomes list in the aforementioned file and create the indicies for the tool listed above.
A more elaborate instruction on how to run this step can be found in the Updating genomes files.
$ ./scripts/bcfngs genomes --help
usage: bcfngs genomes [-h] {check-updates,plan,build} ...

positional arguments:
  {check-updates,plan,build}

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

title: Genome update and mapping workflow
text:
Mapping step
After we nesured, that our needed genomic indices are available for the tool we would like to use, we can as easily run the mapping step for the proper tool using the sam bcfngs mapping command
$ ./scripts/bcfngs mapping --help
usage: bcfngs mapping [-h] {plan,run} ...

positional arguments:
  {plan,run}

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
More elaborate instructions as to how to use this script for mapping the genomes with your favorite tool can be found in the mapping file.

title: Genome update and mapping workflow
text:
Folder structure
To set the path were the genomes can be downloaded to use the genome config file. In our project we’re taking advantage of the pool-folders structire of our institute and store all genomic indices in the pool folder of the ngs core facility (pool-bcfngs).
genomes_root: /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/genomes
Downloading the genomes creates a specific directory per organism, as can be seen below. The folder is split into the separate tools, also keeping the version of the tool that was used. To assertain reproducibility, we can keep previous version of genomics indices, using a softlink to point to the latest version of the indices.
$ tree -L 3 Mus_musculus/
Mus_musculus/
├── bowtie2Index -> .../Mus_musculus/bowtie2Index-2.5.4
├── bowtie2Index-2.5.4
│   ├── Mus_musculus.1.bt2
│   ├── Mus_musculus.2.bt2
...
│   └── Mus_musculus.rev.2.bt2
├── bwaIndex -> .../genomes/Mus_musculus/bwaIndex-0.7.18
├── bwaIndex-0.7.18
│   ├── Mus_musculus.amb
...
│   └── Mus_musculus.sa
├── starIndex -> .../genomes/Mus_musculus/starIndex-2.7.11b
└── starIndex-2.7.11b
    ├── chrLength.txt
    ├── chrNameLength.txt
...
    ├── sjdbList.out.tab
    └── transcriptInfo.tab

title: conda
text:
The first step is to create a conda environment, containing the necessary tools for the required analysis. In this workflow, we use the snakemake package to help running the workflow, as well as some mappers. To learn more about the conda environment and its advantages, take a look at the conda web site.
Environments can be created with the help of a file, e.g. environment.yaml using the following command
conda env create --name Project0815 --file environment.yaml
This file would look like that:
name: Project0815
channels:
   - bioconda
   - conda-forge
   - r
   - bioinfo
dependencies:
   - snakemake-minimal =5.2.4
   - python =3.6
   - star =2.7.2c
   - samtools =1.9
   - bowtie2 =2.3.5
   - bwa =0.7.17
The first row of the file contains the name of the project, i.e. the name of the conda environment created. Next are the channels listed, from which the conda environment should look for the tools needed for the analysis.
the last part of the environment file is a list of the needed dependencies. Each one of them has its version attached to it. This can be very helpful, when a data set needs to be re-analyzed to check for problems or conflicts with older/newer results or for debugging purposes.
After this was done one must activate the environment using the command
conda activate Project0815
If one look at the command line one can now see this
(Project0815) USER@SERVERNAME:Project0815$ ..
The brackets on the left side must contains the name of the environment.
If, after the environment was created, new tools must be added, they can just be added to the list of tools in the environment.yaml file and than the conda environment must be updated, using the command
conda env update -f environment.yaml
The conda environment should be created only once, but with each login one must activate it, so that the snakemake script can be run from the correct paths.

title: conda
text:
The first step is to create a conda environment, containing the necessary tools for the required analysis. In this workflow, we use the snakemake package to help running the workflow, as well as some mappers. To learn more about the conda environment and its advantages, take a look at the conda web site.
Environments can be created with the help of a file, e.g. environment.yaml using the following command
conda env create --name Project0815 --file environment.yaml
This file would look like that:
name: Project0815
channels:
   - bioconda
   - conda-forge
   - r
   - bioinfo
dependencies:
   - snakemake-minimal =5.2.4
   - python =3.6
   - star =2.7.2c
   - samtools =1.9
   - bowtie2 =2.3.5
   - bwa =0.7.17
The first row of the file contains the name of the project, i.e. the name of the conda environment created. Next are the channels listed, from which the conda environment should look for the tools needed for the analysis.
the last part of the environment file is a list of the needed dependencies. Each one of them has its version attached to it. This can be very helpful, when a data set needs to be re-analyzed to check for problems or conflicts with older/newer results or for debugging purposes.
After this was done one must activate the environment using the command
conda activate Project0815
If one look at the command line one can now see this
(Project0815) USER@SERVERNAME:Project0815$ ..
The brackets on the left side must contains the name of the environment.
If, after the environment was created, new tools must be added, they can just be added to the list of tools in the environment.yaml file and than the conda environment must be updated, using the command
conda env update -f environment.yaml
The conda environment should be created only once, but with each login one must activate it, so that the snakemake script can be run from the correct paths.

title: snakemake
text:
This part is still work in progress.
This repository uses snakemake for:
For non-experts, prefer the single wrapper command ./scripts/bcfngs (runs checks, then snakemake).
By default, ./scripts/bcfngs uses the micromamba environment at ~/miniconda3/envs/update_genomes. If your environment is elsewhere, add --env-prefix /path/to/env.

title: snakemake
text:
Configuration files
The workflow configuration is split into two files:

Genome and indexing configuration: configs/genomes.yaml

Organism identifiers are scientific names (e.g. Homo_sapiens, Mus_musculus).
Genome sources are pinned to a specific Ensembl release (at the moment: 115).
Index output directories are versioned via index_versions. These versions are called via the bcfngs script and are not hard-coded here anymore.

Per-project run configuration (mapping): configs/run.yaml

Project ID, input path, organism, threads, etc.

title: snakemake
text:
How-to (cookbook)

Build all genomes + indices
./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20


Build only specific genomes
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --organism Homo_sapiens,Mus_musculus


Ensembl has a newer release

Check:

./scripts/bcfngs genomes check-updates

If an update is available, edit configs/genomes.yaml and bump:


releases.ensembl: 116 → 117 (etc.)


Then:

./scripts/bcfngs genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20


New STAR/bwa/bowtie2 version (rebuild indices)

Update tools:

./scripts/bcfngs env update
./scripts/bcfngs env check

Rebuild with the actual tool version or pin the version label explicitly:
Update configs/genomes.yaml:


set index_versions.star, index_versions.bwa, index_versions.bowtie2 (these strings are used in output folder names)

Index version are now recognized automatically via the bcfngs script.

Rebuild:

./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20
Examples:
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --star-index-version 2.7.11b
./scripts/bcfngs genomes build --cores 20 --star-index-version 2.7.10b --organism Homo_sapiens
The wrapper now requires explicit version labels to match the resolved binary version for that tool.


Use HPC modules for aligners (fallback)
This still uses the micromamba env for python, snakemake, and curl, but resolves aligners from modules:
module load bclconvert/4.3.13 star bowtie2 bwa
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules env check
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes plan
./scripts/bcfngs --profile modules genomes build --cores 20
If you preload an older module version, the wrapper reuses it and prints the resolved tool/version report to stderr. Mapping then requires the matching explicit index version flag, for example --star-index-version 2.7.10b.

title: snakemake
text:
Genome download + indexing
Workflow file: workflow/genomes.smk

Mapping the samples with the STAR
mapping the samples in a paired-end format
This script contains three (four) steps. in the first step the fastq samples are mapped to the indexed genome. Then, they are being indexed. Last, after creating a chromosome-size file, the bam files are converted into bigwig files for better visualization using the UCSC browser.
We have two separate scripts for single-end and paired-end runs. The PE run looks for both R1 and R2 fastq files, while the SE script will ignore all R2 samples.
After the environment was created and activated, one must change to the directory of the concatenated fast files. E.g.
cd /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq/
To run mapping, update configs/run.yaml in the repo, then run one of these Snakefiles.
# for single-end samples
snakemake -nps /path/to/repo/Star.MappingQuant.SingleEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50
# for paired-end samples
snakemake -nps /path/to/repo/Star.MappingQuant.PairedEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50

-n - dry-run. This parameter will only output the command, but execute them. This should be used to test the parameters, but must be removed for the analysis to be done.
-p - Print the commands to be execute.
-s - the name of the snakeake file to be executed.
-j - #cores. How many cores should be used in the run.
